items.name: json
items.description: Parses JSON request bodies into req.body and stores the raw buffer on req.rawBody for signature verification. Auto-detects charset from the Content-Type header. Supports size limits, strict mode, gzip/deflate/brotli decompression, and a verify callback for webhook signatures.

options.notes: Maximum body size (e.g. '10kb', '5mb', or bytes as number).
Reject payloads whose root is not an object or array.
JSON.parse reviver function for custom deserialization.

Content-Type(s) to match. Accepts a string, an array of strings, or a function returning boolean. Supports suffix patterns like 'application/*+json' to match vendor types (e.g. application/vnd.api+json).
verify(req, res, buf, encoding) — called with the raw Buffer before parsing. Throw an error to reject the request with 403. Ideal for webhook signature verification (e.g. Stripe, GitHub).
Decompress gzip, deflate, and brotli request bodies automatically. Set to false to reject compressed bodies with 415.
Reject non-HTTPS requests with 403.

tips: With strict: true (default), primitives like '"hello"' or '42' are rejected — only {} and [] are allowed.
req.rawBody is always set to the raw Buffer before JSON.parse — use it for HMAC signature verification.
Charset is auto-detected from the Content-Type header (e.g. charset=utf-16le). Falls back to utf8.
The type option accepts arrays: ['application/json', 'application/*+json'] matches both standard and vendor JSON types.
Compressed bodies (gzip, deflate, brotli) are automatically decompressed. Set inflate: false to reject them with 415.

items.name: urlencoded
items.description: Parses URL-encoded form bodies (application/x-www-form-urlencoded) into req.body. Supports nested object parsing, parameter limits for DoS prevention, nesting depth limits, and stores req.rawBody for verification.

options.notes: Enable nested bracket parsing (e.g. user[name]=Alice → { user: { name: 'Alice' } }).
Maximum body size.
Maximum number of form parameters. Exceeding this returns 413. Prevents DoS via field flooding.
Maximum nesting depth for bracket syntax (e.g. a[b][c][d]…). Exceeding this returns 400. Prevents deep-nesting DoS.
Content-Type(s) to match.
verify(req, res, buf, encoding) — called with the raw Buffer before parsing. Throw to reject with 403.
Decompress gzip/deflate/brotli bodies. Set to false to reject compressed bodies with 415.
Reject non-HTTPS requests with 403.

tips: parameterLimit: 1000 (default) protects against field-flooding DoS attacks automatically.
depth: 32 (default) prevents deeply nested bracket keys like a[b][c][d]… from consuming CPU.
req.rawBody is set before parsing — use it for signature verification on form submissions.

items.name: text
items.description: Reads the raw request body as a string into req.body. Matches Content-Type text/* by default. Auto-detects charset from the Content-Type header, falling back to the encoding option. Stores req.rawBody for verification.

options.notes: Content-Type(s) to match.
Maximum body size.
Fallback character encoding when Content-Type has no charset parameter.
verify(req, res, buf, encoding) — called with the raw Buffer before decoding. Throw to reject with 403.
Decompress gzip/deflate/brotli bodies. Set to false to reject compressed bodies with 415.
Reject non-HTTPS requests with 403.

tips: Charset is auto-detected from the Content-Type header. The encoding option is only used as a fallback.
req.rawBody is always set to the raw Buffer before string conversion.

items.name: raw
items.description: Reads the raw request body as a Buffer into req.body. Also sets req.rawBody. Useful for binary data, webhooks, custom protocols, and signature verification workflows.

options.notes: Content-Type(s) to match.
Maximum body size.
verify(req, res, buf) — called with the raw Buffer before setting req.body. Throw to reject with 403.
Decompress gzip/deflate/brotli bodies. Set to false to reject compressed bodies with 415.
Reject non-HTTPS requests with 403.

tips: req.rawBody and req.body are both the same Buffer instance for the raw parser.
Use the verify callback for webhook signature validation before processing the payload.

items.name: multipart
items.description: Streams multipart/form-data file uploads to disk and populates req.body with { fields, files }. Supports per-file size limits, file count limits, field count limits, MIME type whitelists, and combined total size limits for upload handling.

options.notes: Upload directory. Created automatically if it doesn't exist.
Maximum size per file in bytes. Rejects oversized files with 413.
Maximum number of uploaded files. Exceeding this returns 413. Prevents file flooding.
Maximum number of non-file form fields. Exceeding this returns 413. Prevents field flooding DoS.
Maximum size of a single field value in bytes (default 1 MB). Exceeding this returns 413.
Maximum combined size of all uploaded files in bytes. Exceeding this returns 413.
Whitelist of allowed MIME types for uploaded files (e.g. ['image/png', 'image/jpeg']). Files with non-matching types are rejected with 415.
Reject non-HTTPS requests with 403.

tips: Files are streamed to disk, not buffered in memory — safe for large uploads.
The upload directory is created automatically if it doesn't exist.
Each file object contains: originalFilename, storedName, path, contentType, size.
Use allowedMimeTypes to restrict uploads to specific file types — rejected files return 415.
maxFiles: 10 (default) prevents file-flooding attacks. Lower it for endpoints that expect a single file.
maxTotalSize limits the combined size of all uploads in a single request — useful for quota enforcement.
